title: RemoteControlFlags
description: CLI flags for `claude remote-control` (server mode), as documented at code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control. claude-box's --remote-serve uses this shape (see lib/remote-control-flags.ts's renderRemoteControlArgs) to build its entrypoint args — one definition instead of ad hoc argv pushes, so the flags actually passed can't silently drift from what's documented/decided. This describes claude's OWN CLI, not anything claude-box invents.

name.description: --name: custom session title visible in the session list at claude.ai/code.

sessionNamePrefix.description: --remote-control-session-name-prefix: prefix for auto-generated session names when no explicit name is set (default: machine hostname). claude-box always sets this to "claude-box" so auto-generated names are identifiable, instead of the meaningless random per-container hostname.

resume.description: Mutually exclusive with spawn/capacity/createSessionInDir (claude itself rejects the combination) — modeled as a separate shape below, not just a comment.

spawn.description: same-dir: sessions share one cwd (can conflict). worktree: each on-demand session gets its own git worktree (requires a git repo) — claude-box's default, matching its own --repo-ephemeral isolation philosophy. session: single-session mode, rejects additional connections; cannot combine with capacity.

capacity.description: Max concurrent sessions. Not usable with spawn=session.

createSessionInDir.description: Pre-create one session in the current directory at startup.

sandbox.description: claude's OWN filesystem/network sandboxing. claude-box leaves this false (the default) — podman is already the real isolation boundary here; turning claude's own sandbox on top of that is redundant at best and could conflict with claude-box's own doors/mounts at worst.

not.description: spawn=session can never combine with an explicit capacity.
